title: Dynamic vs continuous batching
description: Dynamic batching groups whole requests per step; continuous (in-flight) batching swaps finished sequences for new ones each iteration, key for generative models.
body: Use when serving models with variable output length, especially generative ones. Static/dynamic batching forms a batch and runs it to completion, so a long sequence holds the whole batch hostage (head-of-line blocking). Continuous/in-flight batching (as in vLLM) evicts finished sequences and admits new ones each decoding step, keeping the accelerator full and cutting tail latency. Do not use whole-request batching for long, variable-length generation. Actions: pick continuous batching for token-by-token generation. Validation signals include sustained utilization with short requests not blocked by long ones. Risk boundary: whole-request batching for generation wastes capacity and spikes tail latency.
